product_name: EcoShape  bottle of water (0.5 L): Serving of 500 ml of hydration to the consumer. The study examines the extraction and processing of all upstream raw materials to produce the products and packaging components; the manufacturing process; distribution logistics; product use and packaging disposal. The work has been conducted using the Ecoinvent database and SimaPro software.  Climate change impacts are measured in kg CO2 equivalents, following the IPCC’s most recent methodology).  Scenario evaluations have been used to examine the importance of several variables in the product comparison. These include a range of possible consumer behaviours regarding the use of the products, such as conditions of refrigeration, washing of glasses, and disposal routes for packaging, among others.

product_name: Walkers Crisps 32.5 g sold in the United Kingdom
weight_source: As per company's submission to CDP
change_reason_reported: Please note that supplied figures are based on a re-baselined and certified Carbon Footprint with the Carbon Trust reflecting serving size changes.  The date of previous figure supplied reflects the date of the previous certification.  The reduction in our Walkers Crisps product can be attributed to • Electricity and gas efficiency improvements at site as a result of continued reduction targets at site. • Reduction of fuel consumption per km travelled (through vehicle replacements and driver efficiency). • Increased efficiency in logistics network management, resulting in a reduction in less empty return journeys. This included partners with customers. • A secondary packaging (corrugate case) weight reduction of 5% • Increased efficiency of raw material use at sites.  The UK business has made a commitment to reduce the agricultural footprint (carbon & water) of key potato suppliers by 50% in 5 years – the results of this supply chain engagement should be evidenced in the next re-certification.
adjustments: Divided total emissions by 1000, because they were given in grams; Streamlined product name to be uniform across years

product_detail: Walmart Brand Product Emissions (Reported in kg/1000 units)  12-Grain Bread, Natural Wheat Bread, Honey Oatmeal Bread, Yeasty Rolls, and Soft Wheat Rolls
weight_source: Estimated from external data based on product description
change_reason_reported: We produced 9.86% more Walmart-brand products by weight. Since my estimations are based on weight, more Walmart-brand pounds produced will result in an increase in emissions/unit. In addition, and to a lesser extent, my accounting error in 2013 resulted in me under-reporting emissions for 2013. This also had the effect of my emissions relative to 2013 being a little higher. I also had more emissions overall in 2014 than in 2013.
change_reason_category: Model/parameters AND product carbon efficiency changed

product_detail: Walmart Brand Product Emissions (Reported in kg CO2e/lb units)  12-Grain Bread, Honey Oatmeal Bread, Yeasty Dinner Rolls, Seeded Rye Bread, Pumpernickel Bread
weight_source: As per company's submission to CDP
change_reason_reported: We manufactured the same products for Walmart in FY15, so I would expect the emissions to be similar. Our production lbs of Walmart brand products increased 49.3% over FY15, but our emissions per unit stayed relatively constant (-2.07%). Due to mymanufacturing process it is difficult to obtain exact emission numbers for specific Walmart brand products. Thus, I calculate an emissions factor based on company-wide emissions, including all products produced, then multiply this emission factor by the weight of Walmart brand products. Due to this limitation in reporting, by Walmart brand emissions decreased because my emissions for all products decreased.
